Showing 30 companies

A

01 / Developer Platform & APIs

Alchemy

Best forDevelopers who need reliable node infrastructure, APIs, and SDKs to build and scale dApps without managing their own nodes

Gives you the APIs and infrastructure to build blockchain applications without running your own nodes. If you are a development team building a tokenization platform, DeFi protocol, or Web3 application and need reliable, scalable node access with developer tools, Alchemy is the default choice.

#1 Developer PlatformBillions of RequestsMulti-ChainEnhanced APIs
Company intelligence

Alchemy provides the foundational infrastructure that most Web3 applications run on. Rather than managing your own blockchain nodes (which is expensive and operationally complex), you call Alchemy's APIs to read and write to the blockchain. Their platform includes enhanced APIs (NFT, token, transaction history), debugging tools, webhooks for real-time events, and analytics. Powers major applications and processes billions of requests daily. If you are building on Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Solana, or other major chains, Alchemy's node infrastructure and developer tools let your team focus on product rather than infrastructure.

HQ
USA
Founded
2017
Service areas
Node Infrastructure, APIs, SDKs, Analytics
Supported chains
Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Solana, Optimism
Visit alchemy.com
A

02 / Decentralized Node Infrastructure

Ankr

Best forProjects wanting decentralized RPC node access, staking infrastructure, and AppChain deployment without centralized dependencies

Provides decentralized Web3 infrastructure including RPC nodes, staking, and AppChains. If you want node infrastructure that is not dependent on a single centralized provider, or you need to deploy your own application-specific blockchain, Ankr provides the decentralized alternative.

Decentralized RPCMulti-ChainStakingAppChains
Company intelligence

Ankr differentiates from centralized node providers (Alchemy, QuickNode) by offering decentralized RPC infrastructure distributed across independent node operators. This matters for projects that want to avoid single points of failure or centralization risk in their infrastructure layer. Beyond RPC, Ankr provides liquid staking products, AppChain deployment tools, and Web3 gaming infrastructure. If decentralization of your infrastructure layer matters as much as decentralization of your application layer, Ankr is the choice.

HQ
USA
Founded
2017
Service areas
Node Infrastructure, Staking, AppChains
Supported chains
Ethereum, BNB Chain, Polygon, Avalanche, 30+
Visit ankr.com
A

03 / Layer 1 Blockchain

Avalanche (Ava Labs)

Best forEnterprises and developers wanting high-throughput custom blockchains (Subnets) with institutional-grade performance

A Layer 1 blockchain that lets you create custom blockchains (Subnets) with your own rules, validators, and gas tokens. If you are building a tokenization platform that needs its own dedicated blockchain with high throughput but wants the security and ecosystem of an established network, Avalanche Subnets provide that architecture.

Custom SubnetsSub-Second FinalityInstitutionalHigh-Throughput
Company intelligence

Avalanche's unique value is Subnets: you can launch your own blockchain with custom rules (who can validate, which tokens pay gas, what compliance requirements exist) while remaining connected to the broader Avalanche ecosystem. This is particularly relevant for institutional tokenization projects that need dedicated throughput, custom compliance rules, and validator control without building a chain from scratch. Avalanche's consensus mechanism delivers sub-second finality and high throughput. Several major institutions have launched Subnets for tokenized asset projects.

HQ
USA
Founded
2019
Service areas
L1 Blockchain, Custom Chains, Institutional
Supported chains
Avalanche
Visit avax.network
A

04 / Cross-Chain Communication Network

Axelar

Best forDevelopers building multi-chain dApps that need universal messaging and token transfers across 50+ blockchains

Connects 50+ blockchains with universal cross-chain communication. If your tokenized asset needs to exist on multiple chains, or your dApp needs to call contracts on different chains, Axelar provides the messaging layer that makes multi-chain applications possible.

50+ ChainsUniversal MessagingProof-of-StakeCross-Chain
Company intelligence

Axelar provides a decentralized network specifically for cross-chain communication. Unlike bridges that move tokens between two specific chains, Axelar enables general-purpose messaging across 50+ blockchains. You can call a contract on Ethereum from Polygon, transfer tokens from Avalanche to Cosmos, or build applications that span multiple chains seamlessly. For tokenized assets that need multi-chain liquidity or compliance verification across chains, Axelar provides the interoperability infrastructure. Secured by its own proof-of-stake network with a growing validator set.

HQ
Canada
Founded
2020
Service areas
Interoperability, Messaging, Token Transfers
Supported chains
50+ blockchains
Visit axelar.network
B

05 / Account Abstraction & UX Infrastructure

Biconomy

Best fordApp developers wanting to remove Web3 friction (gas fees, seed phrases, transaction signing) for their end users

Removes the pain points of using Web3 applications. If your tokenized asset platform needs end users to transact without understanding gas fees, managing seed phrases, or signing complex transactions, Biconomy's account abstraction SDK makes Web3 feel like Web2.

Account AbstractionGasless TransactionsSmart AccountsSDK
Company intelligence

Biconomy solves the user experience problem that kills Web3 adoption. Their account abstraction SDK enables gasless transactions (you pay gas on behalf of users), smart accounts (users log in with email/social, no seed phrase), batched transactions (multiple actions in one click), and session keys (approve once, transact many times). For tokenization platforms targeting retail or institutional users who should not need to understand blockchain mechanics, Biconomy abstracts away the complexity. Your users interact with your product, not with the blockchain.

HQ
Singapore
Founded
2019
Service areas
Account Abstraction, Gasless, UX Infrastructure
Supported chains
Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base
Visit biconomy.io
C

06 / Blockchain Security & Audit Platform

CertiK

Best forProjects needing fast, comprehensive smart contract audits with ongoing on-chain monitoring and a security leaderboard

The highest-volume blockchain security firm with 4600+ audits completed. If you need a smart contract audit with a recognized brand name, ongoing on-chain monitoring after deployment, and a security score that investors check, CertiK provides the combination of audit + monitoring + reputation.

4600+ AuditsSkynet MonitoringSecurity ScoreFast Turnaround
Company intelligence

CertiK has audited more projects than any other firm in the space (4600+). Their offering goes beyond a one-time audit report: Skynet provides ongoing on-chain monitoring after deployment, tracking for vulnerabilities and suspicious activity. Their CertiK Security Leaderboard has become a reference point that investors and users check before interacting with a protocol. For tokenization projects, a CertiK audit provides both the security review and the reputational signal that your contracts have been professionally reviewed. The trade-off vs boutique firms: breadth and speed vs depth of research.

HQ
USA
Founded
2018
Service areas
Smart Contract Auditing, Monitoring, Security Scoring
Supported chains
Multi-Chain
Visit certik.com
CL

07 / Oracle Network & Cross-Chain Protocol

Chainlink Labs

Best forAny smart contract that needs off-chain data (pricing, NAV, interest rates, identity) or cross-chain token transfers

The standard oracle network that feeds real-world data into smart contracts. If your tokenized asset needs a price feed, NAV calculation, interest rate data, proof of reserves, or cross-chain transfers (via CCIP), Chainlink is the infrastructure that connects your on-chain contracts to off-chain reality.

Dominant OracleCCIP Cross-ChainProof of ReserveData Feeds
Company intelligence

Chainlink is not optional for most tokenization projects. If your smart contract references any external data (asset prices, NAV, interest rates, FX rates, proof of reserves), you need an oracle, and Chainlink is the dominant provider. Their Data Feeds power DeFi's pricing infrastructure. Their Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol (CCIP) enables secure token transfers and messaging between chains. Their Proof of Reserve product verifies that tokenized assets are actually backed. For RWA tokenization specifically, Chainlink's ability to bring verified off-chain data on-chain is fundamental infrastructure.

HQ
USA
Founded
2017
Service areas
Oracles, Cross-Chain, Data Infrastructure
Supported chains
Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, Arbitrum, Solana, 30+
Visit chain.link
C

08 / Competitive Crowdsourced Auditing

Code4rena

Best forProjects wanting multiple independent auditors competing to find vulnerabilities, producing broader coverage than a single firm

Runs competitive smart contract auditing contests where dozens of independent auditors review your code simultaneously. If you want broader security coverage than a single audit firm provides, or you want to complement a traditional audit with crowdsourced review, Code4rena's competition model finds bugs that individual firms miss.

Competitive AuditingCrowdsourcedMultiple AuditorsTransparent
Company intelligence

Code4rena flips the traditional audit model. Instead of hiring one firm, you post your codebase as a contest. Dozens of independent security researchers compete to find vulnerabilities, motivated by bounty payouts for confirmed findings. The competitive dynamic means more eyes on your code from more diverse perspectives than any single firm can offer. Many projects use Code4rena alongside a traditional audit: the firm provides structured review, the contest catches what the firm missed. Results are public, which builds transparency and trust.

HQ
USA
Founded
2021
Service areas
Smart Contract Auditing, Crowdsourced Security
Supported chains
Multi-Chain
Visit code4rena.com
C

09 / Ethereum Ecosystem Builder

ConsenSys

Best forEnterprises and developers building on Ethereum who need the full stack: wallet (MetaMask), infrastructure (Infura), L2 (Linea)

Builds core Ethereum infrastructure that most of the ecosystem depends on. If you are building on Ethereum and need a wallet (MetaMask, 30M+ users), node infrastructure (Infura, used by most Ethereum apps), or an L2 (Linea), ConsenSys provides the foundational tools. Also offers enterprise blockchain solutions.

MetaMask 30M+InfuraLinea zkEVMEnterprise Solutions
Company intelligence

ConsenSys is the largest company building on Ethereum, and their products are used by virtually everyone in the ecosystem. MetaMask is the most-used crypto wallet (30M+ monthly users). Infura provides the node infrastructure that most Ethereum applications rely on. Linea is their zkEVM Layer 2 for scaling. For enterprises, ConsenSys offers private Ethereum networks and consulting services. If you are tokenizing assets on Ethereum, your users will likely interact through MetaMask and your backend will likely route through Infura, whether or not you directly engage ConsenSys.

HQ
USA
Founded
2014
Service areas
Wallets, Node Infrastructure, L2 Scaling, Enterprise
Supported chains
Ethereum, Linea
Visit consensys.io
E

10 / Restaking & Shared Security Protocol

EigenLayer

Best forMiddleware builders and new protocols that need Ethereum-grade security without building their own validator set

Lets new protocols borrow Ethereum's security through restaking. If you are building middleware, an oracle, a bridge, or any protocol that needs a trust layer and do not want to bootstrap your own validator set from scratch, EigenLayer lets you tap into Ethereum's existing validator security.

Restaking PioneerShared SecurityEthereum ValidatorsMiddleware
Company intelligence

EigenLayer introduces restaking: Ethereum validators can opt to secure additional protocols beyond just Ethereum. This means new protocols (oracles, bridges, data availability layers, keeper networks) can access Ethereum-grade security without recruiting and incentivizing their own validator sets. For the RWA tokenization ecosystem, this matters because middleware components (oracles feeding asset prices, bridges moving tokens cross-chain, compliance layers verifying identity) all need security guarantees. EigenLayer provides those guarantees by extending Ethereum's trust to the middleware layer.

HQ
USA
Founded
2021
Service areas
Shared Security, Restaking, Middleware
Supported chains
Ethereum
Visit eigenlayer.xyz
GN

11 / Web3 Automation & Relay

Gelato Network

Best fordApp developers needing automated smart contract execution, gasless relaying, or scheduled on-chain actions

Automates smart contract execution so things happen without someone clicking a button. If your tokenization platform needs automated dividend distributions, scheduled compliance checks, liquidation triggers, or gasless transaction relaying, Gelato runs those automations reliably.

Web3 AutomationRelay NetworkScheduled ExecutionMulti-Chain
Company intelligence

Gelato solves a specific problem: smart contracts cannot execute themselves. Someone or something needs to call the function. Gelato's network of executors monitors conditions and triggers contract calls automatically. For tokenization platforms, this means automated dividend distributions (every 24 hours, distribute rental income), automated compliance checks (verify investor eligibility before each transfer), automated rebalancing (adjust fund allocations based on NAV), and relay services (let users submit transactions without gas). Essential infrastructure for any tokenization platform that needs to feel automated rather than manual.

HQ
Switzerland
Founded
2019
Service areas
Automation, Relay, Gasless Transactions
Supported chains
Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Avalanche
Visit gelato.network
H

12 / Blockchain Security & Pen Testing

Halborn

Best forProjects needing comprehensive security beyond just smart contracts: penetration testing, DevOps security, and infrastructure audits

Provides security audits plus penetration testing, DevOps security, and infrastructure review. If you need more than just a smart contract audit, Halborn reviews your entire stack: contracts, frontend, backend, infrastructure, key management, and operational security.

Full-Stack SecurityPen TestingDevOpsElite Team
Company intelligence

Halborn goes wider than pure smart contract audit firms. Their elite team (many former military/intelligence cybersecurity backgrounds) reviews not just your smart contracts but your entire security posture: web application penetration testing, cloud infrastructure security, key management procedures, DevOps pipeline security, and social engineering resistance. For tokenization platforms where a smart contract bug is only one of many attack vectors (compromised admin keys, frontend exploits, infrastructure breaches), Halborn's full-stack security approach covers the gaps that contract-only auditors miss.

HQ
USA
Founded
2019
Service areas
Smart Contract Auditing, Pen Testing, DevOps Security
Supported chains
Multi-Chain
Visit halborn.com
H

13 / Enterprise Layer 1 (Hashgraph)

Hedera

Best forEnterprises wanting a council-governed, enterprise-grade DLT with Fortune 500 governance and predictable low fees

An enterprise-grade distributed ledger governed by a council of Fortune 500 companies (Google, IBM, Boeing, Deutsche Telekom, others). If you need a blockchain for institutional tokenization where governance is controlled by known, accountable corporations rather than anonymous validators, Hedera provides that trust model.

Fortune 500 Governance$0.001 Fees10K+ TPSCarbon Negative
Company intelligence

Hedera is different from every other chain on this list. Instead of proof-of-stake with anonymous validators, it uses hashgraph consensus governed by a rotating council of up to 39 major corporations. This matters for institutional tokenization: the companies governing the network are identifiable, accountable, and have reputations to protect. Hedera offers predictable, low transaction fees ($0.001 fixed for token transfers), high throughput (10,000+ TPS), and carbon-negative operations. StegX and Zoniqx use Hedera for institutional real estate tokenization via ERC-7518.

HQ
USA
Founded
2018
Service areas
L1 Blockchain, Enterprise, Token Service
Supported chains
Hedera
Visit hedera.com
I

14 / Bug Bounty Platform

Immunefi

Best forProtocols wanting ongoing, incentive-aligned security through 90K+ white-hat researchers hunting for bugs 24/7

Runs the dominant Web3 bug bounty platform with 90K+ security researchers. If you have already been audited but want continuous, ongoing security review where researchers are financially incentivized to find and report vulnerabilities rather than exploit them, Immunefi provides that permanent security layer.

90K+ Researchers$190B+ Protected$100M+ PaidOngoing Security
Company intelligence

Immunefi fills the gap between one-time audits and live exploitation. After your audit is complete and your contracts are deployed, Immunefi's 90K+ security researchers continuously examine your code for vulnerabilities. When they find one, they report it through Immunefi's responsible disclosure process and receive a bounty (you set the amount). This is fundamentally different from an audit: it is ongoing, incentive-aligned, and draws from a far larger pool of researchers. Immunefi has facilitated $100M+ in bounty payouts and protected $190B+ in assets. For tokenization platforms holding real value, a bug bounty is essential ongoing insurance.

HQ
Singapore
Founded
2020
Service areas
Bug Bounties, Ongoing Security, Responsible Disclosure
Supported chains
Multi-Chain
Visit immunefi.com
L

15 / Omnichain Interoperability Protocol

LayerZero

Best forToken issuers wanting their tokenized asset to exist natively on multiple chains with a single deployment

Enables omnichain tokens and applications. If you want your tokenized asset to be natively available on Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, Avalanche, and other chains without separate deployments and bridges, LayerZero's OFT (Omnichain Fungible Token) standard lets you deploy once and extend everywhere.

Omnichain ProtocolOFT Standard50+ ChainsUnified Supply
Company intelligence

LayerZero's value for tokenization is the OFT standard: Omnichain Fungible Tokens. Instead of deploying your token on Ethereum and then bridging copies to other chains (which creates fragmented liquidity and bridge risk), you deploy an OFT that natively exists on all supported chains. When a user moves the token from Ethereum to Arbitrum, it burns on one chain and mints on the other, maintaining a unified supply. For tokenized assets that need multi-chain distribution without the security risks of traditional bridges, LayerZero provides the interoperability layer.

HQ
Canada
Founded
2021
Service areas
Interoperability, Token Standards, Messaging
Supported chains
Ethereum, Arbitrum, Polygon, Avalanche, Solana, 50+
Visit layerzero.network
M

16 / Web3 Data APIs

Moralis

Best forDevelopers needing clean, indexed blockchain data (wallet balances, NFT metadata, token prices, DeFi positions) through simple API calls

Gives you clean, indexed blockchain data through simple API calls. If you are building a tokenization dashboard, portfolio tracker, or analytics tool and need wallet balances, transaction history, token prices, or NFT metadata without running your own indexer, Moralis provides the data layer.

Web3 Data APIsMulti-ChainClean DataDeveloper-First
Company intelligence

Moralis abstracts away the complexity of reading blockchain data. Instead of running your own indexing infrastructure to track wallet balances, transaction history, token transfers, and DeFi positions, you call Moralis APIs and get clean, structured data. Their Web3 Data APIs cover wallet portfolios, token prices, NFT metadata, DeFi positions, and transaction history across multiple chains. For tokenization platforms building investor dashboards, compliance reporting tools, or portfolio views, Moralis provides the data infrastructure so you do not have to build it.

HQ
Sweden
Founded
2020
Service areas
Data APIs, Indexing, Analytics
Supported chains
Ethereum, Polygon, BNB, Arbitrum, Avalanche
Visit moralis.io
N

17 / Ethereum Core Infrastructure & Research

Nethermind

Best forProjects needing deep Ethereum protocol expertise, custom client development, or ZK research and implementation

Builds core Ethereum infrastructure including the Nethermind client (one of the main Ethereum execution clients), research tools, and ZK technology. If you need protocol-level Ethereum expertise, custom client modifications, or cutting-edge ZK proof research applied to your project, Nethermind is the engineering partner.

Ethereum ClientCore InfrastructureZK ResearchProtocol-Level
Company intelligence

Nethermind is one of the teams that builds Ethereum itself. Their Nethermind client is one of the main execution clients running the Ethereum network. This gives them protocol-level understanding that application-layer developers cannot match. They also conduct ZK proof research, build MEV infrastructure, and provide custom blockchain engineering services. For tokenization projects that need deep protocol modifications, custom precompiles, or integration with Ethereum's roadmap (account abstraction, PBS, danksharding), Nethermind provides the engineering depth.

HQ
UK
Founded
2017
Service areas
Core Infrastructure, Client Development, ZK Research
Supported chains
Ethereum
Visit nethermind.io
O

18 / Smart Contract Standards & Security Audits

OpenZeppelin

Best forAny project building smart contracts that needs battle-tested, industry-standard libraries and the gold standard audit firm

Wrote the smart contract libraries your tokenization platform probably depends on. If your contracts import OpenZeppelin (ERC-20, ERC-721, access control, upgradeable patterns), having the team that wrote those libraries audit your modifications is the most logical security investment.

Industry StandardOpen Source LibrariesGold Standard AuditsDefender
Company intelligence

OpenZeppelin occupies a unique position: they write the standard smart contract libraries that most of the ecosystem builds on AND they audit the code that uses those libraries. Their open-source contracts (ERC-20, ERC-721, ERC-1155, access control, upgradeable proxies, governance) are the most deployed smart contracts in existence. Their audit practice reviews whether your custom logic correctly extends and modifies those standards. For tokenization projects using ERC-3643, ERC-7518, or custom token standards, an OpenZeppelin audit means the team that understands the underlying patterns is reviewing your specific implementation. Also offers Defender for operational security monitoring.

HQ
Argentina
Founded
2015
Service areas
Smart Contract Libraries, Security Audits, Operational Security
Supported chains
Multi-Chain
Visit openzeppelin.com
PL

19 / Multi-Chain Scaling Ecosystem

Polygon Labs

Best forProjects needing Ethereum scaling with multiple options: PoS for speed, zkEVM for security, CDK for custom chains

Provides multiple Ethereum scaling solutions so you can pick the trade-off that fits. If you need fast, cheap transactions (Polygon PoS), Ethereum-equivalent security with ZK proofs (zkEVM), or your own custom L2 chain (CDK), Polygon offers all three under one ecosystem.

Multi-SolutionPoS + zkEVM + CDKEthereum ScalingWide Adoption
Company intelligence

Polygon is not a single chain but an ecosystem of scaling solutions. Polygon PoS provides fast, cheap transactions with an established DeFi ecosystem. Polygon zkEVM provides Ethereum-equivalent execution with zero-knowledge proof security. Polygon CDK (Chain Development Kit) lets you build your own ZK-powered L2 chain with custom parameters. For tokenization projects, the choice depends on your needs: PoS for projects wanting existing liquidity and low fees, zkEVM for projects prioritizing Ethereum-level security guarantees, CDK for projects wanting their own dedicated chain with ZK security. Several major tokenization platforms (Securitize, Tokeny) deploy on Polygon.

HQ
India
Founded
2017
Service areas
L2 Scaling, Custom Chains, ZK Proofs
Supported chains
Polygon PoS, Polygon zkEVM, Polygon CDK
Visit polygon.technology
PN

20 / Institutional Market Data Oracle

Pyth Network

Best forDeFi protocols and tokenized asset platforms needing real-time, institutional-grade price data from first-party sources

Delivers institutional-grade market data on-chain from first-party sources (exchanges, market makers, trading firms). If your tokenized asset platform needs real-time pricing with sub-second updates from the same data providers that Wall Street uses, Pyth provides that institutional data feed.

First-Party DataSub-Second Updates450+ FeedsInstitutional
Company intelligence

Pyth differentiates from Chainlink by sourcing data directly from first-party providers: major exchanges, trading firms, and market makers contribute their own proprietary data. This means Pyth's price feeds come from the entities that actually make markets, not from aggregating third-party APIs. Pyth delivers sub-second price updates, which matters for DeFi protocols and tokenized assets that need real-time pricing for trading, liquidation, or NAV calculations. Over 450+ data feeds across crypto, equities, forex, and commodities.

HQ
USA
Founded
2021
Service areas
Oracles, Market Data, Pricing
Supported chains
Solana, Ethereum, 30+
Visit pyth.network

21 / Blockchain Security Audits

Quantstamp

Best forDeFi and tokenization projects needing a security firm with a long track record and $200B+ in value secured

One of the longest-standing blockchain security firms, having secured $200B+ in digital asset value. If you want an audit firm with a proven track record across hundreds of major projects over many years, Quantstamp brings institutional memory and deep experience.

$200B+ SecuredSince 2017Long Track RecordFinancial Focus
Company intelligence

Quantstamp has been auditing smart contracts since 2017, making them one of the most experienced firms in the space. With $200B+ in secured value across projects including major DeFi protocols, L1/L2 chains, and tokenization platforms, they bring a depth of pattern recognition that newer firms have not yet developed. Their audit process combines manual expert review with proprietary automated tools. For tokenization projects, Quantstamp's experience with financial smart contracts (lending, trading, custody) is particularly relevant.

Q

22 / Multi-Chain Node Infrastructure

QuickNode

Best forDevelopers needing fast node spin-up across 25+ chains with low-latency, high-availability infrastructure

Provides multi-chain node infrastructure with fast spin-up across 25+ blockchains. If you need reliable, high-availability node access with low latency and the ability to switch between chains quickly, QuickNode is the speed-focused alternative to Alchemy.

25+ ChainsLow LatencyFast Spin-UpHigh Availability
Company intelligence

QuickNode focuses on speed and reliability for node infrastructure across 25+ blockchains. Their differentiation is fast provisioning (nodes ready in seconds), low-latency responses, and high-availability guarantees. They also offer Streams (real-time data delivery), Functions (serverless compute at the edge), and marketplace add-ons for enhanced functionality. For tokenization platforms that need to support multiple chains or want the flexibility to test and deploy across different blockchains quickly, QuickNode's multi-chain infrastructure and fast provisioning make it easy to expand chain support.

HQ
USA
Founded
2017
Service areas
Node Infrastructure, Streams, Functions
Supported chains
Ethereum, Solana, Polygon, Arbitrum, 25+

S

23 / Audit Coverage with Exploit Insurance

Sherlock

Best forProtocols wanting their audit backed by financial coverage, so if an audited contract gets exploited, there is a payout

Combines smart contract audits with exploit insurance. If you want your audit firm to have financial skin in the game, Sherlock covers audited contracts with exploit payouts. If an audited contract gets hacked, Sherlock pays out from their coverage pool. Aligns incentives between auditor and protocol.

Audit + InsuranceExploit CoverageAligned IncentivesFinancial Backing
Company intelligence

Sherlock solves the misaligned incentive problem in traditional auditing. When you pay a firm for an audit and the contract later gets exploited, the audit firm faces reputational damage but no financial consequence. Sherlock changes this: they audit your contracts AND provide financial coverage against exploits in the audited code. If the audited contract is exploited, Sherlock pays out from their coverage pool. This creates genuine financial alignment: Sherlock's revenue depends on their audits actually preventing exploits. For tokenization platforms where an exploit could mean loss of real-world asset value, this financial backing provides an additional layer of protection.

HQ
USA
Founded
2021
Service areas
Smart Contract Auditing, Exploit Insurance
Supported chains
Multi-Chain
Visit sherlock.xyz
SL

24 / High-Performance Layer 1

Solana Labs

Best forProjects needing ultra-fast transactions (400ms), low fees, and high throughput for consumer-scale tokenized applications

The highest-performance major blockchain with 400ms block times and transaction costs under $0.01. If you are building a tokenized application that needs consumer-scale speed and affordability (think: thousands of transactions per second for micro-investments, real-time trading, or high-frequency operations), Solana delivers the raw performance.

400ms Blocks<$0.01 FeesHigh ThroughputConsumer Scale
Company intelligence

Solana's architecture is optimized for speed and cost at scale. With 400ms block times, sub-cent transaction fees, and theoretical throughput of 65,000 TPS, it handles volume that would be prohibitively expensive on Ethereum L1. For tokenization projects, this matters when your application involves high transaction volumes: frequent dividend distributions, real-time trading of fractional assets, micro-investments, or any use case where per-transaction costs need to be negligible. Ondo Finance, Pyth Network, and several tokenization projects run on Solana for these performance characteristics.

HQ
USA
Founded
2018
Service areas
L1 Blockchain, High Performance
Supported chains
Solana
Visit solana.com
S

25 / ZK-STARK Layer 2

Starknet (StarkWare)

Best forProjects needing provable computation and maximum scalability with post-quantum security guarantees on Ethereum

A ZK-STARK based Layer 2 on Ethereum providing provable computation with no trusted setup. If you need the strongest mathematical security guarantees for your on-chain computations, post-quantum resistant proofs, and the scalability benefits of zero-knowledge technology, Starknet is the most technically rigorous L2.

ZK-STARKNo Trusted SetupPost-QuantumProvable Computation
Company intelligence

Starknet uses STARKs (Scalable Transparent ARguments of Knowledge) rather than SNARKs. The practical difference: STARKs require no trusted setup ceremony, are transparent (verifiable by anyone), and are post-quantum secure. For tokenization projects that need provable computation (verifiably correct NAV calculations, provable compliance checks, transparent settlement), Starknet's ZK proofs provide mathematical certainty that computations were executed correctly. The trade-off is a smaller ecosystem and developer tooling compared to Ethereum or Polygon, but the security properties are unmatched.

HQ
Israel
Founded
2018
Service areas
L2 Scaling, ZK Proofs, Provable Computation
Supported chains
Starknet (Ethereum L2)
Visit starkware.co
T

26 / Smart Contract Development Platform

Tenderly

Best forDevelopment teams needing to simulate, debug, and monitor smart contracts with full transaction visibility before and after deployment

Lets you simulate transactions before sending them, debug failed transactions after they happen, and monitor your contracts in real-time. If your development team needs to test tokenization contract logic against real blockchain state without spending real gas, Tenderly provides the development and monitoring platform.

Transaction SimulatorDebuggerReal-Time MonitoringDevEx
Company intelligence

Tenderly is the development platform that makes building smart contracts faster and safer. Transaction Simulator lets you test transactions against live blockchain state without deploying or spending gas. Debugger gives you step-by-step execution traces of any transaction. Monitoring alerts you in real-time when unusual contract activity occurs. For tokenization platform development teams, this means you can simulate complex token transfer scenarios, debug compliance check failures, and monitor your deployed contracts for unexpected behavior. It is the closest thing to a proper development environment that blockchain development has.

HQ
Serbia
Founded
2018
Service areas
Development Tools, Simulation, Monitoring
Supported chains
Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism, Avalanche
Visit tenderly.co
TG

27 / Decentralized Indexing Protocol

The Graph

Best fordApps needing fast, structured queries of blockchain data through custom subgraphs without running centralized indexing infrastructure

Indexes blockchain data so your dApp can query it efficiently. If your tokenization platform needs to display investor portfolios, transaction history, or compliance records and you do not want to build and maintain your own indexing infrastructure, The Graph's subgraphs provide the queryable data layer.

Decentralized IndexingSubgraphsGraphQLThousands of Indexers
Company intelligence

The Graph solves a fundamental problem: blockchains are not databases. You cannot run SQL queries against a blockchain. The Graph indexes blockchain events into queryable subgraphs that your application can query with GraphQL. For tokenization platforms, this means you can efficiently query: all investors holding a specific token, all transfers in a time period, current balances across all holders, compliance event history, and dividend distribution records. Without The Graph (or similar indexing), you would need to scan the entire blockchain history for this data. The protocol is decentralized with thousands of Indexers processing queries.

HQ
USA
Founded
2018
Service areas
Data Indexing, Querying, Subgraphs
Supported chains
Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Avalanche, 30+
Visit thegraph.com
T

28 / Full-Stack Web3 Development Platform

Thirdweb

Best forDevelopment teams wanting to ship Web3 products fast with pre-built contracts, SDKs, and infrastructure without building from scratch

Gives you pre-built smart contracts, SDKs, and infrastructure so you can launch Web3 products in days instead of months. If you are a development team that wants to ship a tokenization product fast without writing everything from scratch, Thirdweb provides the building blocks.

Pre-Built ContractsMulti-Language SDKsFast ShippingOpen Source
Company intelligence

Thirdweb accelerates Web3 development by providing pre-built, audited smart contracts (ERC-20, ERC-721, marketplace, drops, staking), SDKs for every major language (React, Unity, Python, Go), and hosted infrastructure (IPFS storage, RPC, embedded wallets). For teams building tokenization products, this means you can deploy a token contract, build a frontend, and handle wallet onboarding using Thirdweb's tools rather than building each piece from scratch. The contracts are audited and open-source. The trade-off: you use their patterns and abstractions rather than building fully custom.

HQ
USA
Founded
2021
Service areas
Development Platform, Smart Contracts, SDKs, Infrastructure
Supported chains
Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Solana, 20+
Visit thirdweb.com
TO

29 / Elite Security Research & Audits

Trail of Bits

Best forHigh-value protocols needing the most rigorous, research-grade smart contract and system audits from the industry's top security researchers

The most technically rigorous security firm in blockchain. If your tokenized asset platform holds significant value and you need an audit from researchers who publish original vulnerability research, build security tools used by the entire industry, and set the standard for audit quality, Trail of Bits is the gold standard.

Gold StandardSecurity ResearchOpen Source ToolsElite Team
Company intelligence

Trail of Bits is not just an audit firm. They are a security research organization that happens to audit smart contracts. Their researchers publish original vulnerability research, build open-source security tools (Slither, Echidna, Manticore) used across the industry, and conduct the deepest technical reviews available. A Trail of Bits audit is the highest signal of security seriousness in the ecosystem. The trade-off: they are selective about engagements, have longer lead times, and charge premium rates. For tokenization platforms holding institutional capital, this premium is justified.

HQ
USA
Founded
2012
Service areas
Smart Contract Auditing, Security Research, Tool Development
Supported chains
Multi-Chain
Visit trailofbits.com
W

30 / Cross-Chain Messaging Protocol

Wormhole

Best forProjects needing to send messages and transfer tokens between major blockchains including Ethereum, Solana, and Cosmos ecosystems

Enables cross-chain messaging between major blockchain ecosystems. If your tokenized asset needs to move between Ethereum and Solana, or your application needs to trigger actions on one chain based on events on another, Wormhole provides the messaging infrastructure connecting these ecosystems.

Multi-EcosystemEthereum + Solana19 GuardiansBillions Transferred
Company intelligence

Wormhole connects blockchain ecosystems that do not natively communicate: Ethereum and Solana, EVM chains and Cosmos, Move-based chains and everything else. Their messaging protocol lets developers build applications that span multiple chains, with token transfers as one use case and general message passing as the broader capability. For tokenized assets that need to be available across different blockchain ecosystems (not just different EVM chains), Wormhole provides the bridge. The protocol has processed billions in cross-chain transfers and is backed by a network of 19 Guardian validators.

HQ
USA
Founded
2020
Service areas
Cross-Chain, Messaging, Token Transfers
Supported chains
Ethereum, Solana, Cosmos, Sui, Aptos, 20+
Visit wormhole.com

Selection guide

How to shortlist blockchain development vendors

01

Start with the job to be done

Choose audit, node infrastructure, data indexing, cross-chain, automation, account abstraction or network architecture before comparing brands.

02

Match the chain environment

Confirm support for Ethereum, Polygon, Solana, Avalanche, Hedera, Starknet, Arbitrum, Optimism, Base or multi-chain deployments.

03

Prioritize risk and readiness

For institutional or tokenized asset projects, review security posture, monitoring, operational controls and proof of previous production use.

FAQ

Blockchain Development Vendor Questions

What are the best smart contract audit firms in this category?

OpenZeppelin, Trail of Bits, CertiK, Halborn, Quantstamp, Sherlock and Code4rena are included for smart contract security, auditing, monitoring, bounty or exploit coverage workflows.

Which companies support developer infrastructure and APIs?

Alchemy, QuickNode, Ankr, Moralis, The Graph, Tenderly, Thirdweb, ConsenSys and Nethermind support node infrastructure, APIs, indexing, simulation, SDKs, wallets or core Ethereum tooling.

Which companies support tokenization infrastructure?

For tokenized assets, teams often evaluate Chainlink or Pyth for data, LayerZero, Axelar or Wormhole for cross-chain needs, Polygon, Avalanche, Solana, Hedera or Starknet for network architecture, and security firms before launch.
